title: Simplify global MCP setup docs
summary: The README now presents eve MCP setup as a one-time global/user installation that works across repositories, with hard-coded repository paths moved to fallback guidance.
userVisibleChange: Developers can install eve once, add the MCP server globally to their agent, and rely on the active workspace instead of copying /path/to/repo examples into every configuration.

timeline.title: Removed path-first MCP examples
timeline.summary: Changed Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and generic MCP guidance to prefer global/user stdio setup without hard-coded repository paths.

timeline.title: Clarified repository discovery
timeline.summary: Documented that eve resolves the active Git repository from the MCP server working directory, EVE_CWD, or --cwd as a fallback.

timeline.title: Ran Go tests
timeline.summary: Verified the repository after the README change with go test ./....

decisions.title: Make global stdio the default
decisions.rationale: The expected agent workflow is to install eve once and let each workspace launch the MCP server for its active repository.

decisions.title: Keep --cwd as fallback guidance
decisions.rationale: Some MCP clients may launch stdio servers outside the active workspace, so explicit cwd remains useful as an escape hatch without being the primary path.
